questions.text: The gas law which states that the volume of a given mass of gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature at constant pressure is:
questions.explanation: Charles's Law defines the V-T relationship at constant P.

questions.text: According to the kinetic theory of gases, at absolute zero temperature:
questions.explanation: Absolute zero (0 K) is the theoretical temperature where all molecular thermal motion ceases.

questions.text: The average translational kinetic energy of a gas molecule depends only on its:
questions.explanation: E = (3/2)kT; it is independent of the mass or type of the gas.

questions.text: The number of degrees of freedom for a rigid diatomic molecule is:
questions.explanation: 3 translational + 2 rotational = 5 degrees of freedom.

questions.text: The ratio of specific heats (Cp/Cv) for a monatomic gas is:
questions.explanation: For monatomic gases, γ = 1 + 2/f = 1 + 2/3 = 5/3 ≈ 1.67.

questions.text: Which of the following speed is the greatest for gas molecules at a given temperature?
questions.explanation: v_rms > v_mean > v_mp. Ratio is sqrt(3) : sqrt(8/π) : sqrt(2).

questions.text: The mean free path of gas molecules is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: λ = 1 / (sqrt(2) n π d²), where d is the molecular diameter.

questions.text: If the temperature of a gas is increased from 27°C to 927°C, the rms speed of its molecules becomes:
questions.explanation: v_rms ∝ sqrt(T). T1=300K, T2=1200K. Ratio = sqrt(1200/300) = 2.

questions.text: The value of Universal Gas Constant (R) in SI units is:
questions.explanation: 8.314 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹ is the standard SI value.

questions.text: Dalton's Law of Partial Pressures is applicable to:
questions.explanation: The total pressure is the sum of partial pressures only if gases do not react chemically.

questions.text: Pressure exerted by a gas is due to:
questions.explanation: Change in momentum during collisions creates force/pressure.

questions.text: At constant volume, pressure of a gas is proportional to T. This is:
questions.explanation: Also known as Amontons's Law.

questions.text: A gas behaves more like an ideal gas at:
questions.explanation: Under these conditions, intermolecular forces and molecular volume are negligible.

questions.text: Degrees of freedom for a monatomic gas is:
questions.explanation: Only translational motion in 3D.

questions.text: Internal energy of a real gas depends on:
questions.explanation: Unlike ideal gases, real gases have intermolecular potential energy.

questions.text: Relation between Boltzmann constant (k), R, and Avogadro number (N) is:
questions.explanation: k is gas constant per molecule.

questions.text: According to law of equipartition, energy per degree of freedom is:
questions.explanation: Energy is distributed equally as ½kT per DOF.

questions.text: Mean free path increases with:
questions.explanation: Fewer molecules result in longer distances between collisions.

questions.text: Specific heat Cv for a diatomic gas (ignoring vibrations) is:
The Brownian motion provides evidence for:
questions.explanation: Random motion of particles due to molecular bombardment.

questions.text: Value of Avogadro's number is:
questions.explanation: Standard constant.

questions.text: Molar volume of an ideal gas at STP is:
questions.explanation: Standard molar volume.

questions.text: Vibrational degrees of freedom are active at:
questions.explanation: Vibrational modes require high energy to be excited.

questions.text: Rms speed of hydrogen at T is v. Rms speed of oxygen at T is:
questions.explanation: v ∝ 1/sqrt(M). M_oxy = 16 * M_hyd. Ratio = 1/sqrt(16) = 1/4.

questions.text: In KTG, collisions are assumed to be:
questions.explanation: KE and momentum are conserved in collisions.

questions.text: Kinetic energy of 1 mole of gas is:
Boltzmann constant value is approx:
questions.explanation: Standard physical constant.

questions.text: As pressure of a gas increases at constant T, mean free path:
questions.explanation: Higher density leads to more collisions.

questions.text: Degrees of freedom for non-linear triatomic molecule (e.g. H2O) is:
questions.explanation: 3 translational + 3 rotational = 6.

questions.text: Specific heat Cp for monatomic gas is:
questions.explanation: Cp = Cv + R = 3/2 R + R = 5/2 R.

questions.text: Gamma for diatomic gas is:
Most probable speed v_mp is:
questions.explanation: Speed at the peak of Maxwell distribution.

questions.text: Rms speed v_rms is:
questions.explanation: Calculated from P = 1/3 ρ v².

questions.text: Pressure of an ideal gas P is related to energy density E as:
questions.explanation: From KTG derivations.

questions.text: Volume of 1 mole gas at 0°C and 1 atm is:
questions.explanation: Commonly used value (22.7 for 1 bar).

questions.text: Mean speed is:
questions.explanation: Arithmetic average speed.

questions.text: Total internal energy of n moles of ideal gas is:
questions.explanation: Sum of energy in all degrees of freedom.

questions.text: Maxwell's distribution of speeds depends on:
questions.explanation: Function of (M/2πkT).

questions.text: Mean free path is independent of temperature at constant:
questions.explanation: λ ∝ 1/n; if density is constant, λ is constant.

questions.text: Average momentum of molecules of an ideal gas in equilibrium is:
questions.explanation: Molecules move in all directions equally; vector sum is zero.
